results.title: What are the best alternatives to Amazon's wishlist addon?
results.content:
## Conclusion: which wishlist maker is best?

If you are using Chrome, Firefox, Edge or other desktop browsers: Moonsift has the highest reviews and this was backed up by my testing. It was functionally the best and aesthetically has a clean easy-to-use design.

If you are using Safari desktop: Probably the most reliable option is Giftster for Safari desktop. If you find the interface design too outdated you could also consider Wishlist, Giftwhale or Wishbob.

If you are on Android: I was unable to test Android, but you have options in Things to get me, Giftster, Gift Hero, Wishlist and Wishbob.

If you are on iOS: Moonsift was the standout performer on iOS as well and had the highest overall App Store rating. Second was Things to get me. [...] ## Conclusion: which wishlist maker is best?

If you are using Chrome, Firefox, Edge or other desktop browsers: Moonsift has the highest reviews and this was backed up by my testing. It was functionally the best and aesthetically has a clean easy-to-use design.

If you are using Safari desktop: Probably the most reliable option is Giftster for Safari desktop. If you find the interface design too outdated you could also consider Wishlist, Giftwhale or Wishbob.

If you are on Android: I was unable to test Android, but you have options in Things to get me, Giftster, Gift Hero, Wishlist and Wishbob.

If you are on iOS: Moonsift was the standout performer on iOS as well and had the highest overall App Store rating. Second was Things to get me. [...] ### Moonsift review

Chrome extension: 45 reviews, 5 stars

Moonsift is great for making personal wishlists and gift registries. Saving items from different retailers works perfectly; always picking up the right images, price and description of the products. Moonsift was the only tool I tried that tracked price changes and offered to alert me when prices dropped. Of all the wishlists, it's easily the most aesthetically pleasing. Everything is laid out on a clean canvas and the UX is easy to follow. If you want to use it on mobile there is an iOS app, but unfortunately no Android app yet.

results.title: 10 Best Universal Wishlist Apps 2026 (Ranked & Reviewed) | GiftX
results.content:
What is the best universal wishlist app?

GiftX is the best universal wishlist app because it consolidates items from any online store via paste-link or browser share, then layers AI gift recommendations on top so the list tells gift-givers what to buy. It's available for free on iOS, Android, Telegram, and web (giftx.tech), supports shared family lists with automatic duplicate prevention, and makes it easier for both the wishlist owner and gift-givers. MyRegistry is the best alternative if you prioritize a polished web experience and deep pre-built integrations with major retailers like Target and Macy's.

  What makes a good universal wishlist app? [...] Do you want AI help telling gift-givers what to buy? Most universal wishlist apps stop at consolidation - they show items but don't recommend what's best for the recipient. GiftX uniquely layers AI gift suggestions on top of your list, seeded by recipient profile and budget. If you want to make gift-giving easier for the people buying for you, not just simpler for yourself, GiftX saves them decision fatigue. Other apps like MyRegistry compensate with editorial curation and group gifting, but they don't apply generative AI to personalize recommendations. [...] Where do I download the best universal wishlist apps?

GiftX is available on the Apple App Store (search 'GiftX AI Wishlist'), Google Play (search 'GiftX'), and as a web app at giftx.tech. It also works as a Telegram mini-app at giftx.app. MyRegistry, Moonsift, Giftster, and Wishlistr are all available on iOS and Android via their respective app stores and on the web. Amazon Wishlist is built into the Amazon app and Amazon.com website. All are free to download and use, so you can try multiple apps to see which interface and features you prefer before settling on one.

results.content:
Angie Murphy

 Flag inappropriate

October 24, 2024

I really like this app as an alternative to an Amazon wishlist since the option to add off-site items no longer exist. That being said, one thing I found that I do not care for us when you choose a color option of an item then add it, it doesn't add a link to the color option so it leaves opportunity to to gift someone the wrong thing. I mean sure, you can add it in the description, which is what I've done, but it's not an ideal situation because someone may overlook that detail.

results.title: 10 Best Universal Wishlist Apps 2025 (Ranked & Reviewed)
results.content:
Tips & Tricks

# 10 Best Universal Wishlist Apps 2025 (Ranked & Reviewed)

Discover the top 10 wishlist apps that make gift-giving effortless. From universal store compatibility to seamless sharing, find the perfect app for your needs.

15 min read

By Kristian Connerth

# 10 Best Universal Wishlist Apps 2025 (Ranked & Reviewed)

Our top picks:

 #1 Choice: Wishlists - Best overall with seamless universal compatibility and beautiful design
 #2 Choice: Listful - Solid alternative with comprehensive features and AI-powered recommendations
 #3 Choice: Amazon Wishlist - Perfect for Amazon ecosystem users
 #4 Choice: MyRegistry - Great for event-based registries
 #5 Choice: GoWish - Basic functionality with commercialization concerns

## What Makes a Good Wishlist App [...] ## 6. Elfster

3.5/5

Elfster

Elfster specializes in group gifting but lacks individual wishlist features

Elfster has carved out a strong niche in group gifting and Secret Santa exchanges, serving over 17 million users. The platform excels at organizing gift exchanges with automated name drawing, wish list creation, and gift tracking.

However, its singular focus on group activities makes it a poor choice for individual wishlist management. The interface feels dated compared to modern alternatives, and it lacks the universal store compatibility that makes other apps more versatile.

✅ The Good

 ✅ Best-in-class Secret Santa organization
 ✅ Automatic name drawing and pairing
 ✅ Gift reservation system
 ✅ Works well for group events
 ✅ Free to use

❌ The Bad [...] ❌ The Bad

 ❌ Poor for individual wishlist management
 ❌ Outdated interface design
 ❌ Limited store integration
 ❌ Not ideal for everyday gift lists
 ❌ Focused only on group exchanges
 ❌ Mobile app performance could be better

## 7. Giftster

2.8/5

Giftster

Giftster focuses on families but suffers from outdated design

Giftster positions itself as a family-oriented gift registry with over 2 million users. While it succeeds in group coordination, the platform feels stuck in 2010 with poor mobile optimization and a confusing user interface that frustrates modern users.

The gift reservation system works well to prevent duplicate purchases, but the overall experience is hampered by dated design choices and limited feature set compared to modern alternatives.

✅ The Good

## 6. GoWish — Best for Social Discovery

Best for: Shoppers who like browsing an inspiration feed and sharing lists socially.

GoWish has had a breakout run — north of 13.6 million registered users and more than doubling its user base in 2025, with record daily sign-ups heading into the holidays ( It pairs a universal wishlist with a discovery feed, making it one of the fastest-growing apps in the category.

### GoWish: Key Features [...] In 2020, Lyst attracted over 150 million users and achieved a gross merchandise value exceeding $500 million ( demonstrating the effectiveness of its data-centric model.

### Lyst: Wishlist Functionality

Unlike typical wishlist apps that emphasize collaborative gift planning, Lyst's wishlist focuses on individual shopping needs. It's designed as a personal tool rather than a gift registry — there's no group gifting or reservations. Up next, we'll look at how Pinterest handles wishlist features for broader uses.

Pros: Huge fashion catalog; strong personalization; universal cart for some retailers.

Cons: Fashion-only; its wishlist is a personal shopping tool, not a gift registry.

Price: Free.

## 12. Pinterest — Best for Visual Inspiration [...] Free Group Gifting & Cash Funds: GiftList includes built-in group gifting and cash funds at no cost — and unlike registry platforms that skim a transaction fee, GiftList takes no cut. Contributors pay each other directly through the gift owner's own Venmo, PayPal, Zelle, or Cash App handle, so 100% of every contribution reaches the recipient. That makes it a strong free alternative for big-ticket gifts, group birthdays, weddings, and honeymoon-style funds where competing apps charge a fee.

Key Features and Benefits:
